criteria.name: controls_are_keyboard_accessible
criteria.description: All interactive controls — navigation entries, credential fields and show/hide toggles, filter chips, triage accept/reject/undo actions, stage retry and pause/resume controls, and package copy/download controls — are keyboard accessible with Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, or Space.
criteria.reasoning: Native buttons, inputs, selects, and textareas were rendered for the navigation, triage, pipeline, and package controls. Evidence: initial-shared.png; runs-shared.png

criteria.name: modals_manage_focus
criteria.description: The Connections slide-over, the reject-reason chooser, the delete confirmation, and the command palette trap focus while open, close on Escape, and return focus to the control that opened them.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Connections and reject-reason dialogs returned focus on Escape, but delete confirmation and command palette focus behavior were not fully verified. Evidence: connections-modal.png; reject-modal.png

criteria.name: images_and_icons_have_alt_text
criteria.description: Icons used in the navigation rail, credential rows, stage statuses, triage actions, and package controls have descriptive accessible names or alt text.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered icons were aria-hidden within controls that exposed descriptive accessible names, such as navigation, Connections, show-token, and package actions. Evidence: connections-fields.png; runs-shared.png

criteria.name: feedback_uses_live_regions
criteria.description: A stage entering the failed state, a run completing, and a credential connection status change are each conveyed both visually and via an ARIA live region.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Run completion and credential failure were conveyed through live regions, but a failed-stage transition was not observed. Evidence: runs-shared.png; credential-status.png

criteria.name: forms_have_explicit_labels
criteria.description: All form elements — the credential fields, the AI base URL field, the Add repository field, and the filter controls — use explicit label elements, and the masked-input show/hide toggles carry accessible names.
criteria.reasoning: Credential fields, AI base URL, package import, repository/difficulty/language filters, and masked-input toggles had explicit labels or accessible names. Evidence: connections-fields.png; library-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_follow_logical_order
criteria.description: Headings across Candidates, Triage, Runs, and Library follow a logical order with no skips, such as H1 to H2 to H3.
criteria.reasoning: Observed headings use H1, H2, and H3 levels without skipped levels across Candidates, Triage, Runs, and Library. Evidence: initial-shared.png; triage-current.png; runs-shared.png; library-shared.png

criteria.name: landmark_navigation_is_present
criteria.description: Skip-to-content or landmark navigation is present around the top bar, navigation rail, and main console area.
criteria.reasoning: The rendered app includes banner, complementary navigation rail, named nav landmark, and main landmark. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: text_and_controls_have_contrast
criteria.description: Mode indicator chips, stage status labels, reject-reason badges, and body text have sufficient color contrast against their backgrounds.
criteria.reasoning: Mode/status chips, pipeline stage labels, badges, and body text were visibly readable against their backgrounds in the rendered desktop UI. Evidence: runs-shared.png

criteria.name: semantic_html_roles_are_used
criteria.description: Semantic HTML roles such as nav, main, and button are used for the rail, main view, and interactive controls.
criteria.reasoning: The rendered structure uses native buttons, nav, main, banner, complementary, regions, and dialog roles. Evidence: initial-shared.png; connections-modal.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_is_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, streaming text still appears but transitions apply instantly and nothing pulses or slides, matching the motion specification.
criteria.reasoning: In the reduced-motion browser, prefers-reduced-motion matched true, streaming pipeline text appeared, and computed transition/animation durations were absent while running. Evidence: reduced-run.png

criteria.name: multi_facet_reload_round_trip
criteria.description: In demo mode: set the source-file bounds to 4 and 9, reject one PR with the docs-only reason, run one clean seeded PR to a completed package, and dismiss one coachmark, then reload the page. The bounds, the triage placement with its reason badge, the produced package with its content, and the coachmark dismissal all survive the reload, while both credential rows show Disconnected and the mode indicator reads Demo data — never a mix of kept and reverted facets among the persisted set
criteria.reasoning: Reload preserved bounds 4–9, Demo data, GitHub/AI off, triage cards with Docs only and No linked issue badges, and the completed driftline #58 package; the dismissed connection coachmark stayed absent. Evidence: multi_facet_reload_round_trip-candidates.png, multi_facet_reload_round_trip-triage.png

criteria.name: filter_bounds_derive_from_live_data
criteria.description: Note the PRs visible for driftline at the default bounds 3 to 10, tighten the source-file bounds to 5 and 7 and confirm the visible list shrinks to exactly the PRs whose displayed non-test source-file counts fall between 5 and 7, then restore 3 and 10 and confirm the original list returns exactly — proving the filter recomputes from live data rather than swapping hardcoded lists
criteria.reasoning: Default 3–10 showed 7 PRs; changing to 5–7 showed exactly #58 (6), #54 (5), and #46 (7); restoring 3–10 returned the original seven. Evidence: filter_bounds_derive_from_live_data-shared.png

criteria.name: triage_breakdown_tracks_reasons
criteria.description: Reject one PR with the docs-only reason and another PR with the no-linked-issue reason: the per-reason breakdown increments exactly the docs-only row after the first action and exactly the no-linked-issue row after the second, while the other reason rows stay unchanged — the breakdown is sensitive to which reason was chosen
criteria.reasoning: After real UI rejection actions, Docs only and No linked issue each incremented to 1 while the other reason rows remained 0. Evidence: triage_breakdown_tracks_reasons-final.png

criteria.name: run_echoes_into_library_and_palette
criteria.description: Complete a pipeline run for an accepted PR, then without reloading open the Library and the command palette: the new package appears as a library entry with matching repository and PR number, and searching the palette by that PR number surfaces the package result
criteria.reasoning: Completed driftline #58 appeared in Library, and searching the command palette for 58 returned both the PR and matching library package. Evidence: run_echoes_into_library_and_palette-shared.png

criteria.name: count_deltas_are_exact
criteria.description: Measure the accepted count immediately before and immediately after accepting one PR (delta exactly plus one), and the library entry count immediately before and immediately after deleting one package (delta exactly minus one); the header stats and the list contents agree at both measurement points with no off-by-one or lagging counter
criteria.reasoning: Accepted changed from 3 to 4 after accepting one PR; deleting a library package changed the visible library from 3 entries to 2, with list contents matching both states. Evidence: count_deltas_are_exact-shared.png

criteria.name: different_prs_produce_different_packages
criteria.description: Run the pipeline on two different seeded PRs from different repositories: the two packages differ in the ways the inputs dictate — different PR numbers, base SHAs, and source-file lists in the metadata and patch note, and instruction text reflecting each PR's own linked issue or title rather than shared boilerplate
criteria.reasoning: Completed packages for driftline #58 and petrel #31 had different PR numbers, base SHAs, source-file lists, issue/title-specific instructions, and repository-specific patch notes. Evidence: different_prs_produce_different_packages-shared.png

criteria.name: interleaved_run_and_triage_stay_coherent
criteria.description: Start a pipeline run, switch to the Triage view while Evaluate is still streaming and reject one PR with a reason, then return to the Runs view: the run's stages and streamed output are intact and progressing (or paused exactly where left), and the triage placement and stats change made mid-run is retained — neither flow corrupted the other
criteria.reasoning: While driftline #57 Evaluate was streaming, Triage accepted a Docs only rejection; returning to Runs preserved the run’s Fetch/Evaluate/Generate/Package output and completion, while Triage retained the rejection and updated breakdown. Evidence: interleaved_run_and_triage_stay_coherent-runs.png, interleaved_run_and_triage_stay_coherent-triage.png

criteria.name: library_empty_then_repopulated
criteria.description: Delete every library package (including the seeded examples) and confirm the library shows its empty state with the entry count at zero, then complete a new pipeline run and confirm the library repopulates with exactly the new package, its filters and counts tracking the transition in both directions
criteria.reasoning: After deleting all visible packages, Library showed No packages match with no active filters; a new completed driftline #58 run then repopulated it with exactly one package. Evidence: library_empty_then_repopulated-shared.png

criteria.name: export_import_round_trip_preserves_package
criteria.description: Complete a demo pipeline run, Download bundle the TaskPackageBundle JSON, delete that library entry, Import the downloaded JSON, and confirm the restored entry's viewer shows the same instruction text, repo, pr_number, difficulty, and patch_note as before the delete — proving export and import share one field contract
criteria.reasoning: Downloaded the driftline #58 bundle, deleted the entry, imported the downloaded JSON, and observed matching instruction, repository/PR, medium difficulty, metadata, and patch note. Evidence: export_import_round_trip_preserves_package-shared.png

criteria.name: demo_mode_default_populated
criteria.description: On first load with no credentials entered, a clearly visible mode indicator in the top bar reads Demo data, and the Candidates view lists the 3 seeded repositories (nimbusworks/driftline, cobalt-labs/loomdb, fernfield/petrel) each showing its name, primary language, and merged-PR count
criteria.reasoning: Demo data indicator and all 3 seeded repositories with language and merged-PR counts were visible. Evidence: demo_mode_default_populated-shared.png

criteria.name: connections_panel_masked_credentials
criteria.description: The Connections control opens a slide-over panel containing a GitHub token field, an AI base URL field prefilled with https://api.nimbus-ai.com, and an AI API key field; both key fields are masked by default and each has a show/hide toggle that reveals the value only while active
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The panel and masked fields were observed, and GitHub reveal worked, but the AI show/hide toggle was not exercised end-to-end. Evidence: connections_panel_masked_credentials-shared.png

criteria.name: credential_connect_lifecycle
criteria.description: Entering a GitHub token (any value works for this probe) and activating Connect triggers a visible checking status on that credential row, followed by either Connected with an account login or a visible error carrying the failure status or message with the row remaining Disconnected; after a failed check the fixture repositories, PRs, and packages remain in place and every demo flow still works
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Connect produced a visible 401 error and preserved demo data, but the checking status was not directly observed. Evidence: connections_panel_masked_credentials-shared.png

criteria.name: pr_list_columns_and_paging
criteria.description: Selecting a seeded repository lists its merged pull requests (at least 8 exist per seeded repo) showing per PR the number, title, merged date, a linked-issue indicator, the changed-file count, and a non-test source-file count; the list loads in pages of 5 with a visible control to load the next page and a running count of loaded versus total PRs
criteria.reasoning: PR rows showed numbers, titles, dates, issue indicators, changed-file counts, source counts, and a visible 5-of-7 page with Load next page. Evidence: pr_list_columns_and_paging-shared.png

criteria.name: source_file_filter_and_issue_toggle
criteria.description: The source-file filter with minimum and maximum bounds (defaults 3 and 10) narrows the PR list to PRs whose non-test source-file count is inside the bounds, and the require-linked-issue toggle further narrows to PRs with a linked issue; active filters render as removable chips and clearing them restores the full list exactly
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Bounds, linked-issue filtering, and removable chips worked, but Clear all did not restore the original unfiltered state in the observed workflow. Evidence: pr_list_columns_and_paging-shared.png

criteria.name: pr_detail_changed_files_test_exclusion
criteria.description: Opening a PR's detail shows the full title, body text, the linked issue with number and title (or an explicit No linked issue notice), and a changed-files table listing each filename with its additions and deletions; files in test directories or with test-style filenames are visibly tagged as test files and are excluded from the PR's source-file count
criteria.reasoning: Detail showed title, body, linked issue, additions/deletions, a tagged test file, and 6 non-test source files out of 7 changed. Evidence: pr_detail_changed_files_test_exclusion-shared.png

criteria.name: triage_columns_and_reject_taxonomy
criteria.description: The Triage view shows Inbox, Accepted, and Rejected columns; Accept moves a card to Accepted, Reject requires choosing exactly one reason from too-few-files, too-many-files, docs-only, and no-linked-issue before completing, the rejected card shows its reason as a labeled badge, and the Rejected column can be filtered by reason
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Inbox, Accepted, Rejected, accept, reject reason selection, and reason badge were observed, but rejected-by-reason filtering was not completed. Evidence: triage_columns_and_reject_taxonomy-shared.png

criteria.name: triage_stats_derive_live
criteria.description: The triage header stats (total candidates, accepted count, rejected count, and the per-reason rejected breakdown) update immediately after each accept or reject action without a reload, and the displayed counts match the actual cards in each column
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Stats changed immediately and column totals matched, but the full per-reason breakdown update was not directly verified. Evidence: triage_stats_derive_live-shared.png

criteria.name: triage_undo_reverts_exactly
criteria.description: After an accept or reject, an Undo affordance (toast or inline control) is available for at least the most recent action; activating it returns the card to its previous column and reverts the header stats to their prior values exactly
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Undo appeared after acceptance, but activating it and verifying exact restoration was not completed. Evidence: triage_columns_and_reject_taxonomy-shared.png

criteria.name: pipeline_four_stages_with_statuses
criteria.description: Starting Run pipeline from an accepted PR shows four stages in order — Fetch, Evaluate, Generate, Package — each advancing visibly through pending, running, and complete or failed; a retrying stage shows a retrying status with an attempt counter such as attempt 2 of 3
criteria.reasoning: Fetch, Evaluate, Generate, and Package appeared in order with complete/running/pending states, and retries showed attempt counters. Evidence: pipeline_four_stages_with_statuses-shared.png

criteria.name: evaluate_streams_and_ends_with_verdict
criteria.description: The Evaluate stage streams its assessment text progressively (text appears incrementally, not all at once) and ends with a visible verdict of substantial or trivial plus a one-line reason citing the PR's non-test source-file count against the 3-to-10 window
criteria.reasoning: Evaluate text was observed partial while running, then completed with a substantial verdict and a reason citing 4 files within the 3-to-10 window. Evidence: pipeline_four_stages_with_statuses-shared.png

criteria.name: generate_streams_instruction_text
criteria.description: The Generate stage streams the task instruction text as it is produced, the text references the selected PR's linked issue or title content, and the final streamed text is what appears as the package's instruction document
criteria.reasoning: Generate text appeared partial while running and completed with instruction text referencing the PR title, issue, repository, and source files. Evidence: pipeline_four_stages_with_statuses-shared.png

criteria.name: stage_retry_resumes_from_failure
criteria.description: A failed stage surfaces its error inline with a status code and message plus a Retry control, and activating Retry resumes the run from that stage: fernfield/petrel PR 31 fails Generate across 3 attempts with visible backoff, is marked failed with an inline error summary, and completes after a manual Retry with the Fetch and Evaluate outputs and timestamps unchanged
criteria.reasoning: Petrel PR 31 failed Generate after 3 attempts with 503/backoff details; Retry Generate resumed from Generate while Fetch/Evaluate outputs remained unchanged and the run completed. Evidence: pipeline_four_stages_with_statuses-shared.png

criteria.name: rate_limit_countdown_auto_retry
criteria.description: Running nimbusworks/driftline PR 58 in demo mode hits a simulated rate limit on Evaluate's first attempt showing a 429 status and a visible reset countdown that ticks down; when the countdown reaches zero the stage retries automatically, the attempt counter increments, and the run succeeds
criteria.reasoning: PR 58 showed 429, a ticking reset countdown, retrying 1/3, then automatic retry with attempt 2 and continued progression. Evidence: rate_limit_countdown_auto_retry-shared.png

criteria.name: pause_resume_and_event_timeline
criteria.description: A running pipeline can be paused (stage progression freezes) and resumed (it continues from exactly the same stage with earlier outputs unchanged), and the run's event timeline lists stage transitions in order with timestamps, supports filtering by status, and selecting a timeline entry highlights the corresponding stage
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Pause and Resume controls plus a timestamped/filterable timeline were visible, but exact stage freezing and unchanged outputs across pause/resume were not fully verified. Evidence: pipeline_four_stages_with_statuses-shared.png

criteria.name: trivial_verdict_ends_run_without_package
criteria.description: Running a PR whose Evaluate verdict is trivial (for example the seeded docs-only PR) ends the run after Evaluate with a visible trivial outcome naming the reason, and no package is produced for that run
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The docs-only PR 52 session start did not expose a visible trivial run, so the no-package end state could not be exercised. Evidence: pipeline_four_stages_with_statuses-shared.png

criteria.name: package_four_parts_and_difficulty
criteria.description: A completed run's package viewer shows four parts — the instruction document, a TOML-shaped task config, a metadata summary with repository, PR number, base commit SHA, language, difficulty, and source-file count, and a bug-patch placeholder note naming the PR's non-test source files — and the difficulty matches the source-file count mapping (3 to 4 easy, 5 to 7 medium, 8 to 10 hard) with the count shown
criteria.reasoning: Completed package viewer exposed instruction, task config, metadata, patch note, and bundle parts; metadata showed easy difficulty for 4 source files. Evidence: package_four_parts_and_difficulty-shared.png

criteria.name: package_copy_and_downloads_work
criteria.description: The package viewer offers per-part copy controls that give a visible copied confirmation, individual file downloads for the instruction document and the task config, and a Download bundle control that downloads one TaskPackageBundle JSON file whose visible keys include schemaVersion exactly live-task-package-v1, repo, pr_number, base_sha, language, difficulty, source_file_count, created_at, instruction, task_config, and patch_note
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Copy confirmation and download controls were visible, but individual downloads and bundle JSON download contents were not actually exercised. Evidence: package_four_parts_and_difficulty-shared.png

criteria.name: batch_run_and_bucketed_report
criteria.description: The batch composer queues 2 or more accepted PRs and runs them sequentially with per-item stage progression under an n-of-m progress indicator; the finished batch shows a run report placing every queued PR in exactly one bucket among packaged, trivial, failed, and skipped, with per-bucket counts summing to the batch total, and the report downloads as BatchRunReport JSON with total, packaged, trivial, failed, skipped, and items
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Four-item sequential batch completed with 3 packaged and 1 skipped, but report download was not exercised. Evidence: batch_run_and_bucketed_report-shared.png

criteria.name: library_listing_filters_and_reexport
criteria.description: The Library lists every produced package plus at least 2 seeded example packages showing repository, PR number, difficulty, language, and created date; filters by repository, difficulty, and language combine as removable chips; opening an entry reopens the full package viewer with working copy and download controls; deleting asks for confirmation and removes exactly that entry; re-export downloads the stored TaskPackageBundle content
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Listing, combined removable filters, viewer, confirmation delete, and re-export control were observed, but re-export download was not completed. Evidence: package_four_parts_and_difficulty-shared.png

criteria.name: import_bundle_validated_per_field
criteria.description: The Import bundle control adds a valid TaskPackageBundle JSON (schemaVersion live-task-package-v1 and all required fields) to the library, while an invalid bundle — missing required fields, schemaVersion other than live-task-package-v1, or violating field formats — is rejected with per-field errors naming each offending field and adds nothing
criteria.reasoning: Invalid {} was rejected with named per-field errors; a valid live-task-package-v1 bundle was then imported and opened as a library package. Evidence: import_bundle_validated_per_field-invalid.png

criteria.name: export_import_round_trip
criteria.description: After a completed demo pipeline run, downloading the TaskPackageBundle and importing that same JSON reconstructs a library entry whose viewer shows the same instruction text, repo, pr_number, difficulty, and patch_note as the pre-import package
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: A valid bundle was imported, but the imported instruction was shortened rather than verified identical to the pre-import package. Evidence: import_bundle_validated_per_field-shared.png

criteria.name: command_palette_and_coachmarks
criteria.description: Pressing Cmd+K or Ctrl+K opens a command palette searching repositories, PRs by number or title, and library packages with results grouped by kind; selecting a result navigates to it and Escape closes the palette; on first run dismissible coachmark tips point out the Connections panel, the Triage board, and the pipeline runner, each dismissing individually, and a Reset tips control in the Connections panel restores them
criteria.reasoning: Command palette grouped repositories, PRs, and library packages; selecting a result navigated, Escape closed it, and coachmarks plus Connections Reset tips were visible. Evidence: command_palette_and_coachmarks-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_use_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles, they use consistent capitalization (one convention — title case or sentence case — throughout).

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they are specific verbs ('Run pipeline', 'Download bundle', 'Reject PR'), not generic ('Submit', 'OK') when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Some actions use generic labels such as “Next” and “Queue” where more specific labels would be possible. Evidence: desktop-shared.png, triage-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders error messages, they name the problem and the fix (for example the repository field's required owner/name shape, or the bundle field that failed validation), not just a rejection ('Invalid').

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or marketing copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places, terminology is consistent — for example package versus task package, and Reject PR / reject reason wording does not drift across triage and stats.
criteria.reasoning: Repeated concepts use stable terminology: source files, PR/pull request, accepted/rejected, and bounded reject reasons remain consistent across views. Evidence: desktop-shared.png, triage-shared.png, library-shared.png

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders numbers, dates, and units — merged dates, source-file counts, attempt counters, and n-of-m batch progress — formatting is consistent.
criteria.reasoning: Counts, source-file units, dates, and timestamps use consistent formatting across Candidates, Triage, Library, and Runs. Evidence: desktop-shared.png, triage-shared.png, library-shared.png

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation and success messages, they state what happened (for example Copied, package complete, or undo restored), not vague affirmations.
criteria.reasoning: The success toast explicitly states “PR accepted” and identifies the repository, PR number, and destination state. Evidence: success-shared.png
